note: Snapshot taken immediately before table retirement (deletion) per Myke 2026-07-18.

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D3.4 | Parent: D3 — Grid & Regulatory

Covers long-distance transmission expansion as the structural bottleneck on where and how fast data-center load can be served: FERC Order 1920 long-term regional transmission planning and cost allocation, interregional transfer capability, National Interest Electric Transmission Corridor (NIETC) designations, and major backbone projects (Grain Belt Express, SunZia, MISO LRTP). Transmission adequacy increasingly determines which interconnection-queue positions can actually deliver firm capacity, making FERC transmission policy a leading multi-year indicator of siting viability. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-007 (New Energy Stack). Cross-link: D3.1 (Interconnection Queue & Grid Access).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — DC Power Delivery at the Rack

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D2.4 | Parent: D2 — Power Architecture

The rack-level implementation of 800V DC power delivery: bus bar design, hot-swap power shelf architecture, GPU-native DC-DC converters, and the elimination of per-rack UPS and PDU conversion stages. Distinct from D2.1 (facility-wide 800V DC transition) in that this sub-domain covers the design choices inside the rack enclosure — where the NVIDIA NVLink and OCP Open Rack Wide specifications meet the power chain. Eaton's acquisitions of power electronics specialists and Vertiv's rack power portfolio expansion are the primary company signals. Linked Themes: T-002 (Rack Revolution), T-001 (Power Reckoning).
cellValuesByFieldId.fldgbcIajoAHM1ASi: DC Power Delivery at the Rack

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Lender Insurance Requirements & Risk Transfer

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D21.4 | Parent: D21 — Insurance & Risk Markets

Construction all-risk, builders risk, surety bonds, title insurance for powered land, contractual risk allocation in DC leases. Lender-mandated coverage is a gating item for debt closes on DC construction and acquisition financing. Cross-link: D4 (capital markets).

Linked Themes: T-001, T-004.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Major Outage Postmortems

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D23.1 | Parent: D23 — Outage Intelligence & Emergency Response

AWS us-east-1-style events, hyperscaler outage RCAs, root cause patterns, customer-side impact tracking. The outage-learning loop is one of the few places where architectural mistakes become public knowledge before they become standard practice.

Linked Themes: T-001, T-005.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — GPU Utilization & Compute Scheduling Economics

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D9.2 | Parent: D9 — Orchestration Intelligence & Control Plane

Covers GPU utilization economics: run:ai scheduling, GPU cluster efficiency benchmarks, multi-tenancy models for inference workloads, and the revenue vs. cost implications of utilization improvement. GPU utilization is the primary lever between a profitable AI infrastructure business and an unprofitable one at any given hardware cost basis. Linked Themes: T-005 (Inference Economy).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — EU Media Coverage

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D22.2 | Parent: D22 — Industry Media & Analyst Coverage

DCD Europe, Mission Critical Magazine EU, and regional trade press across Germany, France, UK, Netherlands, Ireland, and the Nordics. EU DC journalism has become structurally more regulatory than U.S. coverage.

Linked Themes: All themes (horizontal coverage).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Hyperscaler Custom Silicon

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D5.2 | Parent: D5 — Hyperscaler Activity

Covers hyperscaler-developed custom AI chips: AWS Trainium/Inferentia, Google TPU v5/v6, Meta MTIA, Apple Neural Engine, Microsoft Maia. Infrastructure focus: internal deployment substitution rate vs. NVIDIA purchases, rack power requirements, and the strategic implication of chip independence for co-location vendors and GPU infrastructure investors. Linked Themes: T-005 (Inference Economy).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Security Operations & Incident Response

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D16.6 | Parent: D16 — Cyber & Physical Security and Resilience

SOC operations, incident response practice, tabletop exercises, breach disclosure, forensic capability. The operational discipline that turns alerts into action. Boundary rule: incident-as-outage-event lives at D23.3; incident-as-practice lives here. Candidate status — seed pending formal review.

Linked Themes: T-006.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Utility Rate Cases & PUC Proceedings

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D3.3 | Parent: D3 — Grid & Regulatory

Covers public utility commission proceedings directly relevant to data center development: large-load rate classification cases, special rate class designs, ratepayer cost shift disputes, demand response obligation frameworks, and utility infrastructure cost recovery decisions. PUC outcomes in VA, TX, GA, PA, NY, OH are among the most consequential near-term regulatory variables for AI infrastructure economics. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-003 (Consent Crisis), T-007 (New Energy Stack).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Interconnection Queue & Grid Access

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D3.1 | Parent: D3 — Grid & Regulatory

Covers ISO/RTO interconnection queues (PJM, MISO, ERCOT, CAISO, NYISO, SPP) as the primary constraint on data center development timelines. Tracks queue volume, average processing times, large-load study requirements, and the FERC Order 2023 reform implications. Queue position and study outcomes are the single most reliable leading indicator of campus delivery risk. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-003 (Consent Crisis).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Software Supply Chain & Vendor Risk

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D16.5 | Parent: D16 — Cyber & Physical Security and Resilience

SolarWinds-class supply chain attacks, SBOM requirements, third-party risk management, firmware integrity, dependency auditing. Supply chain as the fastest-growing threat vector for DC operators. Candidate status — seed pending formal review.

Linked Themes: T-006.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Sovereign Wealth & Institutional Capital

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D4.3 | Parent: D4 — M&A & Capital Markets

Covers sovereign wealth fund direct investment in AI infrastructure: ADIA, PIF (Saudi Arabia), GIC (Singapore), CPPIB (Canada), Temasek, and QIA (Qatar). Tracks commitment sizes, co-investment structures, preferred geographies, and the geopolitical motivations behind infrastructure capital flows. Linked Themes: T-004 (Capital Concentration), T-006 (Sovereign AI Race).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Federal Tax Policy

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D19.2 | Parent: D19 — Tax, Incentives & Fiscal Policy

IRA clean energy credits (45Y, 48E, 45X, 45V), CHIPS Act 48D, Section 174 R&D capitalization, bonus depreciation, Opportunity Zones. Federal tax policy is the structural backdrop that state and local incentives layer on top of.

Linked Themes: T-004.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — DC REIT Performance & Valuation

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D4.2 | Parent: D4 — M&A & Capital Markets

Covers publicly traded data center REITs (Equinix, Digital Realty, Iron Mountain) — earnings, NAV, leasing spreads, development pipeline, and debt market dynamics. Also tracks private platform valuations as a read-across. REIT performance is a leading indicator for private market deal pricing and LP appetite. Linked Themes: T-004 (Capital Concentration).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Tax-Driven Deal Structuring

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D19.5 | Parent: D19 — Tax, Incentives & Fiscal Policy

DC REIT tax treatment, like-kind exchanges, tax-efficient capital stack construction, M&A tax structures. Tax engineering is a material component of deal pricing and platform valuation. Cross-link: D4 (capital markets).

Linked Themes: T-004.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Conference Intelligence & Speaking Circuit

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D8.2 | Parent: D8 — People & Signals

Covers DCD Connect, GTC, OCP Summit, AFCOM, iMasons Member Summits, and similar. Tracks speaker lineups as company priority signals, panel topic evolution as market maturity indicators, and sponsor patterns as competitive intelligence. Conference intelligence is one of the most reliable real-time signals for where industry consensus is forming vs. fracturing. Linked Themes: All themes (cross-cutting).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Physical Security & Access Control

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D16.3 | Parent: D16 — Cyber & Physical Security and Resilience

Biometrics, video analytics, intrusion detection, drone defense, perimeter security, mantraps. Physical security as a distinct discipline from cyber — the facility envelope is its own threat surface. Candidate status — seed pending formal review.

Linked Themes: T-001.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Behind-the-Meter Generation (BYOP/BYOG)

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D2.2 | Parent: D2 — Power Architecture

Covers Bring Your Own Power (BYOP) and Bring Your Own Generation (BYOG) strategies where data center operators develop or contract dedicated generation assets rather than relying solely on grid interconnection. Includes nuclear SMR offtakes, gas peakers, large-scale solar+storage, fuel cells (Bloom Energy, EaaS), and the regulatory and permitting implications of BTM generation at hyperscale. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-007 (New Energy Stack).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Carbon Accounting & Scope 1-3 Reporting

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D11.2 | Parent: D11 — Sustainability

Covers GHG Protocol Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions reporting for data centers: reporting frameworks (GRI, TCFD, CDP), embodied carbon in hardware, scope 3 supply chain disclosure, and the evolution of regulatory reporting requirements. The convergence of investor ESG mandates and regulatory disclosure obligations is making carbon accounting a first-order operational function. Linked Themes: T-003 (Consent Crisis).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Clean Energy Procurement (PPA/REC/CFE)

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D11.1 | Parent: D11 — Sustainability

Covers Power Purchase Agreements, Renewable Energy Certificates, Virtual PPAs, and 24/7 Carbon-Free Energy matching. Tracks hyperscaler clean energy procurement strategies, additionality debates, and the gap between annual REC matching and hourly CFE matching. Clean energy procurement is both a sustainability obligation and a long-term energy cost hedging strategy for operators. Linked Themes: T-007 (New Energy Stack).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Integrated Infrastructure Vendor M&A

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D4.4 | Parent: D4 — M&A & Capital Markets

Covers acquisition activity by major infrastructure vendors (Eaton, Vertiv, Schneider Electric, Siemens, ABB) targeting capabilities across thermal management, DC power electronics, and modular construction delivery. The convergence thesis in T-002 (The Rack Revolution) is visible in M&A before it is visible in product roadmaps — when Vertiv acquires a CDU specialist and a modular construction company in the same fiscal year, that is the signal. Tracks deal rationale, capability gaps being addressed, and the emerging integrated infrastructure stack each vendor is assembling. Linked Themes: T-002 (Rack Revolution), T-004 (Capital Concentration).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Site Selection Criteria & Market Scoring

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D14.1 | Parent: D14 — Real Estate & Site Selection

Covers the multi-factor site selection process: power access, zoning, water availability, fiber connectivity, climate risk, labor markets, and incentive programs. Tracks land banking strategies, MOU signals with utilities, powered shell economics, and the shift from incentive-driven to power-driven siting decisions. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-003 (Consent Crisis).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Chip Export Controls & BIS Regime

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D15.1 | Parent: D15 — Sovereign AI & Geopolitics

Covers BIS export control rule changes (H100/H200/Blackwell restrictions), Entity List additions, CFIUS data center reviews, and the downstream impact on non-US hyperscaler capacity access. Export controls are the fastest-moving geopolitical variable affecting AI infrastructure market structure globally. Linked Themes: T-006 (Sovereign AI Race).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Tax Incentive Backlash & Clawback

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D19.4 | Parent: D19 — Tax, Incentives & Fiscal Policy

Good Jobs First incentive tallies, state clawback legislation, community tax equity arguments, CBA tax offset negotiations. The political economy of data center tax breaks is shifting from automatic to contested. Cross-links: D13 (community relations), D18 (opposition register).

Linked Themes: T-003.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery Practice

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D23.4 | Parent: D23 — Outage Intelligence & Emergency Response

DR site strategy, multi-region failover, runbook evolution, regulatory DR requirements. The evolution from backup to continuous multi-region architecture as a baseline expectation for AI-class workloads.

Linked Themes: T-001.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — OT / ICS / SCADA Cybersecurity

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D16.2 | Parent: D16 — Cyber & Physical Security and Resilience

Operational technology cyber — BMS, DCIM, power distribution controls, cooling system SCADA. CISA ICS advisories, OT network segmentation, vendor risk. Boundary rule: D16 owns threat/practice; D20 owns facility ops tooling itself. Candidate status — seed pending formal review.

Linked Themes: T-001, T-006.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — GPU Architecture & Roadmap

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D1.1 | Parent: D1 — Chips & Density

Covers NVIDIA GPU generational transitions (H100 → B200 → GB300 → Vera Rubin → Feynman), competing silicon (AMD MI-series, Google TPU, AWS Trainium/Inferentia, Meta MTIA), and custom ASIC development. The primary lens is infrastructure impact: power draw, rack density requirements, memory bandwidth, and thermal envelope per generation. Linked Themes: T-002 (Thermal Reckoning), T-005 (Inference Economy).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Executive Movement & C-Suite Intelligence

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D8.1 | Parent: D8 — People & Signals

Tracks C-suite and VP-level moves across operators, hyperscalers, PE platforms, and technology vendors. Covers appointments, departures, promotions, and advisory roles. Executive movement is a leading indicator of platform-level strategic pivots, M&A activity, and competitive positioning changes. Primary sources: LinkedIn, proxy filings, press releases. Feeds AUTO-046 (Executive Role Change Detector). Linked Themes: T-004 (Capital Concentration).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — U.S. Media Coverage

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D22.1 | Parent: D22 — Industry Media & Analyst Coverage

Data Center Dynamics US, Data Center Knowledge, Bisnow, and trade press. General business media DC beats at WSJ, Bloomberg, NYT, FT. U.S. media is the primary lens through which the DC story gets constructed for capital markets and policy audiences.

Linked Themes: All themes (horizontal coverage).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Direct-to-Chip Liquid Cooling (DLC)

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D7.1 | Parent: D7 — Cooling & Water Technology

Covers cold plate and direct-to-chip liquid cooling systems for GPU and CPU packages: Vertiv CDU, CoolIT, Motivair, JetCool, Asetek. Tracks deployment economics, GPU compatibility (NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 thermal specs), and CDU market share dynamics. DLC is the fastest-growing cooling segment by deployment volume for AI clusters. Linked Themes: T-002 (Thermal Reckoning).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Sovereign AI Programs & National Compute

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D6.2 | Parent: D6 — New Entrants

Covers government-backed and state-adjacent AI infrastructure programs: Saudi Arabia’s Humain, UAE’s G42, India’s National AI Mission, EU AI Gigafactory program, Japan’s NEDO compute initiative, and similar. Distinct from D15 in that the focus here is the operator/new entrant dimension — who is building and operating the infrastructure, not the geopolitical/export control overlay. Linked Themes: T-006 (Sovereign AI Race).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Climate & Parametric Risk Products

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D21.3 | Parent: D21 — Insurance & Risk Markets

Weather parametric triggers, flood/wildfire/hurricane coverage, climate transition risk, insurability-zone mapping. Parametric products are how capital markets are pricing climate-driven DC siting risk. Cross-links: D11.3 (water), D11.5 (sustainability regulation).

Linked Themes: T-003.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Long-Lead Equipment & Supply Chain

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D10.1 | Parent: D10 — Construction

Covers transformer (T&D), switchgear, generator, chiller, and CDU lead times as the primary construction bottleneck. Tracks major manufacturers (ABB, Hitachi, Siemens, Eaton, Caterpillar), delivery timeline trends, order backlogs, and the supply chain implications of simultaneous global buildout. Long-lead equipment procurement decisions are now being made 3–5 years in advance at hyperscale. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — APAC / LATAM / MENA Media Coverage

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D22.3 | Parent: D22 — Industry Media & Analyst Coverage

DCD APAC and regional press across Japan, Korea, Singapore, India, Australia, China. LATAM (Brazil, Mexico, Chile). MENA (UAE, Saudi). Africa (Johannesburg). Future split into D22.5 APAC if volume justifies it (OQ-CL-08).

Linked Themes: All themes (horizontal coverage).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Data Center Property & Casualty Insurance

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D21.1 | Parent: D21 — Insurance & Risk Markets

Large-format property coverage, business interruption, FM Global and peers, equipment breakdown, fire, flood. The insurability of multi-hundred-megawatt facilities is becoming a sourcing constraint distinct from availability of capital.

Linked Themes: T-001.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Competing Silicon & Alternative Accelerators

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D1.3 | Parent: D1 — Chips & Density

Covers non-NVIDIA AI accelerators with material infrastructure implications: AMD MI300X/MI350/MI400 series, Google TPU v5/v6, AWS Trainium2/Inferentia3, Meta MTIA, and custom ASICs from Cerebras, Groq, Tenstorrent, Etched. Infrastructure focus: rack power requirements, memory architecture, cooling compatibility, and deployment economics vs. NVIDIA baseline. Linked Themes: T-005 (Inference Economy).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Big 5 Capex & Campus Announcements

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D5.1 | Parent: D5 — Hyperscaler Activity

Covers quarterly capex guidance, actuals, and revisions from AWS, Azure, Google, Meta, and Oracle. Tracks campus announcements (location, MW scale, power source, COD timeline), Stargate and equivalent coalition programs, and the delta between announced and delivered capacity. The primary demand signal for the entire AI infrastructure market. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-004 (Capital Concentration).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Rack Density & Power Density Progression

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D1.2 | Parent: D1 — Chips & Density

Tracks the density roadmap from legacy 10 kW/rack through 30 kW, 80 kW, and 120 kW+ configurations. Covers OCP Open Rack Wide (ORW) standards, the Density Dividend (Jevons Paradox applied to AI compute), facility design implications, and the forcing function relationship between rack density and cooling technology selection. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-002 (Thermal Reckoning).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Neocloud Infrastructure

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D6.1 | Parent: D6 — New Entrants

Covers GPU-as-a-service and cloud compute platforms built around rented or owned AI accelerator clusters rather than traditional IaaS: CoreWeave, Nebius, Lambda, Nscale, Crusoe, and emerging platforms. Tracks funding, capacity commitments, hyperscaler customer relationships, and the competitive positioning vs. Big 5 cloud. Linked Themes: T-004 (Capital Concentration), T-005 (Inference Economy).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — International Tax & Transfer Pricing

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D19.3 | Parent: D19 — Tax, Incentives & Fiscal Policy

Ireland 12.5%, Singapore and UAE regimes, EU state aid rules, OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax, India GST on DC services. Governs cross-border data center structures and where international capacity lands.

Linked Themes: T-006, T-004.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — State Moratorium & Legislative Landscape

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D3.2 | Parent: D3 — Grid & Regulatory

Covers the 2025–2026 state legislative wave: moratorium bills (14 states active, Maine LD 307 enacted), tax incentive rollbacks, water disclosure mandates, and large-load rate class legislation. Tracks bill status, partisan dynamics, and passage likelihood. Primary feed from MultiState Insider, Good Jobs First, DataCenterBans.com, and Data Center Watch. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-003 (Consent Crisis).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Water Use & Waterless Cooling Strategies

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D7.3 | Parent: D7 — Cooling & Water Technology

Covers WUE (Water Usage Effectiveness) metrics, evaporative cooling alternatives, closed-loop adiabatic systems, waterless cooling technologies (air economization, rear-door heat exchangers), and water purchase agreements. Increasingly scrutinized as AI cluster deployments strain municipal and watershed water resources. Covers ZutaCore and the Carrier Global incumbent link as a case study in strategic investment tracking. Linked Themes: T-002 (Thermal Reckoning), T-003 (Consent Crisis).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — State & Local Tax Incentives

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D19.1 | Parent: D19 — Tax, Incentives & Fiscal Policy

Property tax abatements (VA, TX, GA, AZ), sales tax exemptions on IT equipment, gross receipts tax treatment, PILOTs, TIF districts, and enterprise zone designations. State-by-state tax regime variation is a primary site selection input. Cross-links: D3 (regulatory), D13 (community relations CBA tax equity), D14 (site selection).

Linked Themes: T-003, T-004.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Modular & Prefabricated Data Center Delivery

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D10.3 | Parent: D10 — Construction

Covers the shift from bespoke, stick-built data center construction to factory-prefabricated modular delivery: skid-based power modules, containerized cooling units, prefabricated electrical switchgear assemblies, and full Prefabricated Modular Data Centers (PMDCs). Tracks vendor landscape (Vertiv, Schneider, Delta, BladeRoom, Compass, IO Data Centers), timeline compression economics vs. stick-built, quality control advantages, and the limits of modular at extreme density (120+ kW/rack). The modular model is the construction methodology enabler for the 800V DC and DLC transitions — factory integration of MEP systems is significantly easier than field installation. Linked Themes: T-002 (Rack Revolution), T-001 (Power Reckoning).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Enterprise IT Cybersecurity & Cloud Security

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D16.1 | Parent: D16 — Cyber & Physical Security and Resilience

Enterprise IT cyber coverage at DC operators and hyperscalers: identity, network segmentation, cloud workload protection, zero-trust architecture. Candidate status — seed composition to be finalized during D16 formal review (awaiting Myke sign-off).

Linked Themes: T-006.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Cyber Incidents Targeting DC Infrastructure

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D23.3 | Parent: D23 — Outage Intelligence & Emergency Response

Ransomware on DC operators, supply chain attacks, state-actor DC targeting, incident response practice. Cyber-incident-as-outage-event — the threat itself at D16; this tracks the operational consequence. Cross-link: D16.

Linked Themes: T-006.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Facility DCIM & Digital Twin

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D20.2 | Parent: D20 — Facility IT & Operational Technology

Nlyte, Sunbird, Schneider EcoStruxure DC, NVIDIA Omniverse for DCs, Cadence Reality DC. Facility-level DCIM distinct from compute-side orchestration at D9.1 — this tracks power, thermal, and capacity management at the cabinet and room level.

Linked Themes: T-001, T-002.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Project Opposition Register

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D18.1 | Parent: D18 — Community Opposition & Regulatory Risk

Covers project-level tracking: developer, location, MW scale, opposition group(s), project status (Blocked/Delayed/Advanced), dollar value at risk, CBA status, and moratorium exposure. Sources: Data Center Watch, datacenterwatch.org report, local news. Feeds Community Opposition Registry table and AUTO-049. The project register is Faraday’s primary differentiating intelligence asset for D18. Linked Themes: T-003 (Consent Crisis).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — 800V DC Power Distribution

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D2.1 | Parent: D2 — Power Architecture

Covers the transition from 415V AC to 800V DC power distribution within data center facilities. Includes physics and economics of the transition, NFPA code gap timeline, AC-to-DC retrofit risk matrix (the 4×4), OCP 800V DC standards, and the market for DC-native power infrastructure vendors. The single most significant structural change in data center power design in a generation. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-007 (New Energy Stack).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Immersion Cooling

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D7.2 | Parent: D7 — Cooling & Water Technology

Covers single-phase and two-phase immersion cooling systems: Submer, Iceotope, LiquidStack, Green Revolution Cooling, Asperitas. Tracks total cost of ownership vs. DLC, fluid chemistry and safety standards, facility design requirements, and the use case boundary (immersion vs. DLC) as rack densities increase. Linked Themes: T-002 (Thermal Reckoning).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — GC/EPC Capacity & Construction Execution

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D10.2 | Parent: D10 — Construction

Covers GC/EPC capacity constraints, modular and offsite construction adoption, commissioning throughput, and contracting model evolution (design-build, CM-at-risk). Tracks Mortenson, Turner, DPR, and emerging specialist contractors. Construction execution is now a strategic differentiator — operators who can reliably deliver on schedule command a leasing premium. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning).

Sub-Domain ID: D3.5 | Parent: D3 — Grid & Regulatory

Covers the fast-emerging regulatory frontier of how very large data-center loads connect and contract for power: novel large-load tariff designs, bring-your-own-generation (BYOG) interconnection rules, behind-the-meter co-location of data centers with generation, and the FERC/PJM Talen–Amazon Susquehanna co-location ruling that reset the rules for grid-adjacent siting. Also tracks minimum-take and curtailment obligations, special contracts, and the ratepayer cost-allocation disputes they trigger — the terms that determine whether co-located and large-load projects are economic. Linked Themes: T-001 (Power Reckoning), T-007 (New Energy Stack). Cross-links: D2.2 (Behind-the-Meter Generation / BYOP-BYOG), D5.1 (Big 5 Capex & Campus Announcements).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Jurisdiction Posture Intelligence (JPS)

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D18.2 | Parent: D18 — Community Opposition & Regulatory Risk

The Faraday Jurisdiction Posture Score (JPS) is a proprietary 0–100 composite score tracking each jurisdiction's posture toward data center development across five dimensions: (D1) Development Track Record, (D2) Political & Governing Body Posture, (D3) Community & Opposition Dynamics, (D4) Utility & Infrastructure Posture, (D5) Regulatory & Legislative Environment. The unit of analysis is the jurisdiction with zoning authority — County, Township, City, Municipality, or Borough — not necessarily the state. Posture Labels: Champion (75–100) / Favorable (55–74) / Neutral (35–54) / Cautious (15–34) / Hostile (0–14). Momentum tracks directional change: Accelerating / Stable / Decelerating / Inflection. JPS override is permitted with mandatory written rationale. Seed set: 10 US jurisdictions scored as of April 2026, anchored by documented project outcomes. Data table: Jurisdiction Posture Registry (tblAZB4CjCBGHREKi). This is Faraday’s most differentiated D18 intelligence asset — it converts reactive opposition tracking into predictive site selection intelligence. Linked Themes: T-003 (Consent Crisis).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Building Management Systems & HVAC Control

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D20.1 | Parent: D20 — Facility IT & Operational Technology

Siemens Desigo, Johnson Controls Metasys, Honeywell, Schneider EcoStruxure Building. BMS as facility operations layer — the tooling that runs HVAC, power distribution monitoring, and environmental control at the building level.

Linked Themes: T-001, T-002.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — PE & Infrastructure Fund Activity

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D4.1 | Parent: D4 — M&A & Capital Markets

Covers private equity and infrastructure fund deployment into data center platforms: acquisitions, platform builds, minority stakes, and exits. Primary tracked entities: Blackstone, DigitalBridge, KKR, Brookfield, Blue Owl, Ares, TPG, Macquarie, Goldman Sachs Alternatives. Focus on deal structure (club deals, sale-leaseback, build-to-suit), return expectations, and hold period dynamics. Linked Themes: T-004 (Capital Concentration).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Grid Events & Physical Disasters

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D23.2 | Parent: D23 — Outage Intelligence & Emergency Response

Extreme weather impact on DCs, grid failures affecting campuses, ERCOT-style winter storm events, wildfire evacuations. Cross-links: D3.1 (interconnection), D11.3 (water).

Linked Themes: T-001, T-003.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Industry Analysts

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D22.4 | Parent: D22 — Industry Media & Analyst Coverage

Gartner, IDC, Synergy Research, Dell'Oro, 451 Research / S&P Global, Omdia, TBR. CRE analysts: Cushman & Wakefield DC, JLL DC, Newmark, Structure Research. Analyst reports and rankings shape enterprise buying narratives and platform valuations.

Linked Themes: All themes (horizontal coverage).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — UPS, Storage & Power Conditioning

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D2.3 | Parent: D2 — Power Architecture

Covers uninterruptible power supply architecture evolution (VRLA to lithium-ion to flywheel), battery energy storage systems (BESS) for grid stabilization and demand response, and power conditioning equipment. Particular focus on lithium-ion adoption economics, fire suppression implications, and the role of UPS in BTM generation architectures. Linked Themes: T-007 (New Energy Stack).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — State-Sponsored & Advanced Persistent Threats

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D16.4 | Parent: D16 — Cyber & Physical Security and Resilience

APT tracking (Mandiant, CrowdStrike), state-actor campaigns targeting DC infrastructure, attribution reporting, CISA KEV catalog, geopolitical threat modeling. Candidate status — seed pending formal review.

Linked Themes: T-006.

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — AI Factory Orchestration & DCIM

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D9.1 | Parent: D9 — Orchestration Intelligence & Control Plane

Covers the NVIDIA DSX AI Factory reference architecture, NVL72/NVL144 cluster management, DCIM platform evolution (Nlyte, Schneider EcoStruxure, Vertiv), and software-defined power and cooling management. Infrastructure focus: how orchestration decisions determine sustained power draw, cooling activation patterns, and facility capex requirements. Linked Themes: T-005 (Inference Economy).

fld1T0irjU2kWItCc.name: Coming Soon — Cyber Insurance & Tech E&O

cellValuesByFieldId.fldDjJailiG2IzSWW:
Sub-Domain ID: D21.2 | Parent: D21 — Insurance & Risk Markets

Standalone cyber coverage, ransomware exclusions, AI-liability coverage, state-actor exclusion debates. Home for cyber-as-insurance-product; the threat itself lives at D16. Cross-link: D16.

Linked Themes: T-004, T-006.
